channel.title: RC42 Community Contributions
channel.description: Latest community contributions reviewed and published by ISA RC42 Social Psychology.

item.title: Principles of Social Psychology Open Textbook
item.description:
Principles of Social Psychology is an open educational resource for introductory social psychology courses. It covers major areas of social psychology and can be used by instructors and students as a free teaching and learning resource.
Source: Open Textbook Library / University of Minnesota

item.title: Towards Reflexive and Context-Sensitive Research in Personality and Social Psychology
item.description:
Frontiers in Psychology is accepting manuscripts for a Research Topic on reflexive and context-sensitive research in personality and social psychology. The topic welcomes theoretical, empirical, methodological, and mixed-methods contributions that address culture, power, positionality, generalisability, and the links between individual dispositions and social processes.
Source: Frontiers in Psychology

item.title: Cyberpsychology and Social Dynamics in the Connected World
item.description:
Frontiers in Psychology is accepting manuscripts for a Research Topic on cyberpsychology and social dynamics in digitally connected environments. The call focuses on online social interaction, algorithms, AI, identity, digital inequalities, online violence, data ethics, and digital social justice.
Source: Frontiers in Psychology

item.title: SPSP 2027 Annual Convention Call for Submissions
item.description:
The Society for Personality and Social Psychology has opened the call for submissions for the SPSP 2027 Annual Convention. The call invites submissions for podium presentations, posters, and roundtable unconferences, offering an international opportunity for scholars, students, and early-career researchers working in personality and social psychology.
Source: Society for Personality and Social Psychology

item.title: Call for Papers: Special Issue on Social Status
item.description:
Social Psychology Quarterly invites submissions for a special issue on social status. The call welcomes empirical and theoretical articles or research notes on how status is determined, how it affects individuals, groups, and society, and how it relates to inequality, power, emotions, identity, conflict, decision-making, workplaces, classrooms, and methodological advances in the study of status.
Source: Social Psychology Quarterly / American Sociological Association

item.title: Call for Papers: The Social Psychology of Third Parties in Intergroup Conflicts
item.description:
The journal Social Psychology invites submissions for a special issue on the role of third parties in intergroup conflicts. The issue welcomes theoretical and empirical contributions on how actors not directly involved in conflicts may shape conflict dynamics through mediation, commentary, allyship, sanctioning, bystander involvement, reconciliation, collective memory, and social justice.
Source: Social Psychology / European Association of Social Psychology

item.title: Call for Papers: Anti-LGBTQ+ Policies Worldwide
item.description:
The Journal of Community and Applied Social Psychology invites submissions for a special issue on anti-LGBTQ+ policies worldwide. The issue focuses on psychosocial processes, community impacts, policy climates, social norms, intergroup contact, symbolic threat, collective action, resilience, intersectionality, and applied interventions across cultural contexts.
Source: Journal of Community and Applied Social Psychology / European Association of Social Psychology

item.title: Conference Call: Combating Harmful Stereotyping
item.description:
The conference “Combating harmful stereotyping: a cross-disciplinary approach” invites abstracts on stereotypes and generalizations across fields including social psychology, sociology, communication studies, linguistics, philosophy of language, political science, organizational behaviour, anthropology, and cognitive psychology.
Source: European Association of Social Psychology / Université Grenoble Alpes

item.title: Call for Papers: Social Processes from a Cultural Perspective
item.description:
Social Influence invites submissions for the article collection “Social Processes from a Cultural Perspective.” The collection welcomes research on social connection, belonging, loneliness, social norms, conformity, influence, and related phenomena across diverse cultural and cross-cultural contexts.
Source: Social Influence / Taylor & Francis / European Association of Social Psychology
